evals.name: self-base-styling
evals.prompt: I'm adding a new `Tag` component to `@agentic-ui/react`. Its `styles.js` has a `useTagStyle()` hook that returns the base look (flat decls plus an `_hover` rule). How should I apply that to the `Box` in `Tag.js` so that a consumer can still override it? Should the base styles go in `sx` or `__sx`? Show the call site.
evals.expected_output: Recommends __sx (not sx) for self base styling; shows composeSx(styleProps, __sxProp) folded after {...rest}; destructures __sx out of rest; notes sx is consumer-only and wins over base.

evals.name: child-override-wrapper
evals.prompt: My `SplitButton` component renders a `<Button>` and needs to override Button's hover background, while still letting an app developer's `sx` win over my override. Where do I put SplitButton's override styles, and why not just use `sx`? Show how it composes with Button's own base.
evals.expected_output: Routes the wrapper override through __sx (passed into Button's incoming __sx), folded via composeSx; explains the child folds incoming __sx after its own base so the override wins at equal specificity while consumer sx (tier 4) still wins; explains why sx would require a fragile hand-merge.

evals.name: debug-dropped-override
evals.prompt: A consumer passes `sx={{ _hover: { color: 'red' } }}` to our `MenuItem`, but the component's own hover styling wins and red never applies. Inside MenuItem we do `sx={{ ...menuItemSx, ...props.sx }}`. What's wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Identifies two faults: (1) the component authors its own styling on sx (should be __sx, consumer-only channel), and (2) object-merge {...a, ...b} replaces the whole _hover key instead of array composition. Fix: move menuItemSx to __sx via composeSx, let consumer sx flow natively; or at minimum compose arrays not object-merge.
